name: MS-QRD-001: Excessive Failed Authentication — Credential Brute Force
description: Detects a high volume of authentication failures originating from a single source IP within a 5-minute window. Aggregates failed login events across all log sources including Windows Security (EventID 4625), Linux PAM, and network device authentication logs. A threshold of 10+ failures per source IP indicates brute-force or credential stuffing activity.
author: MilliSec Blue Team — Rihad Ebilov

qradar.aql: SELECT LOGSOURCENAME(logsourceid) AS "Log Source", sourceip AS "Source IP", CATEGORYNAME(category) AS "Event Category", COUNT(*) AS "Event Count", SUM(eventcount) AS "Total Failed Attempts", MIN(DATEFORMAT(starttime, 'yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss')) AS "First Seen", MAX(DATEFORMAT(starttime, 'yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss')) AS "Last Seen", QIDNAME(qid) AS "Event Name" FROM events WHERE CATEGORYNAME(category) ILIKE '%authentication failure%' OR CATEGORYNAME(category) ILIKE '%failed login%' OR CATEGORYNAME(category) ILIKE '%logon failure%' OR CATEGORYNAME(category) ILIKE '%invalid user%' OR CATEGORYNAME(category) ILIKE '%login failed%' GROUP BY sourceip, LOGSOURCENAME(logsourceid), CATEGORYNAME(category), QIDNAME(qid) HAVING SUM(eventcount) >= 10 ORDER BY "Total Failed Attempts" DESC LAST 5 MINUTES
qradar.offense_relevance: High volume of authentication failures from a single source indicates automated brute-force attack. Correlate with successful login (EventID 4624 / category: Authentication Success) from the same source IP to detect successful compromise after brute force.
response_actions: Check if any of the targeted accounts had subsequent successful logins
Add source IP to QRadar Reference Set: Blocked_IPs
qradar.false_positive_notes: Legitimate service accounts performing batch operations, misconfigured applications with incorrect credentials, password change propagation delays in AD environments.
qradar.tuning_notes: Adjust HAVING threshold (default: 10) based on baseline authentication failure rate in your environment. Consider increasing to 20-30 in high-volume AD environments.
